_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize SW Florida House batch (HD-72/74/75/76/77): Conerly, Buchanan, Nix, Oliver, Esposito. State tier. Buchanan currency: not seeking House reelection, filed for FL Senate SD-22 (HD-74 open 2026). Esposito: cleared uncited party-inference TRUEs; only HARD-cited cells kept.

profile.background: U.S. Navy veteran (1984-89), civil engineer (PE). Fifth-generation Floridian, Lakewood Ranch. First elected to FL House HD-72 in Nov 2024 (def. Lesa Miller). 2026 R primary vs John Phillips.

bill-conerly@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06 (state tier). Freshman (2024). HARD conservative votes: YEA CS/HB 289 (2026) fetal personhood/wrongful-death; YEA HB 991 (2026) proof-of-citizenship to register/vote; YEA HB 133 (2026) restoring 18-21 long-gun purchase. Florida Right to Life endorsed (2024). Campaign site: expanded E-Verify to all FL employers, supports local LEO immigration enforcement/deportation. No cross-party deviations found. Marriage/family/Christian-liberty/federalism returned NO EVIDENCE (freshman; marquee FL social bills predate his tenure) -> null, not inferred.

0.note: YEA CS/HB 289 (2026) fetal personhood/wrongful-death bill; Florida Right to Life endorsed 2024.

4.note: FRTL-endorsed; no Planned Parenthood/abortion-industry PAC funding found.

1.note: YEA HB 991 (2026) requiring proof of U.S. citizenship to register to vote.

0.note: Campaign site explicitly backs local law enforcement enforcing immigration laws; pro-police affirmative advocacy.

1.note: Campaign site: gives local LEO means to deport illegal aliens; cuts funding for programs benefiting illegal aliens.

3.note: Campaign site: expanded E-Verify to ALL Florida employers.

1.note: YEA HB 133 (2026) rolling back post-Parkland 21-yr long-gun age limit to 18 -> opposing a gun restriction.

profile.background: Real estate broker; FSU (Finance), USF (MBA). Sitting FL House HD-74 since 2018. NOT seeking House reelection in 2026 -- filed to run for FL Senate District 22 (succeeding term-limited Joe Gruters); HD-74 becomes an open seat. Son of retiring US Rep. Vern Buchanan (distinct person).

james-buchanan@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06 (state tier). CURRENCY: Buchanan is sitting HD-74 but is NOT seeking House reelection -- he filed to run for FL Senate District 22 (Aug 18 2026 R primary, reportedly sole filer; backed by Senate GOP leadership). HD-74 will be OPEN in 2026. Scored on his real House record. Freedom Index cumulative 73%. HARD votes: YES SB 300 (2023 6-wk ban), HB 5 (2022 15-wk), SB 404 (2020 parental consent); co-introducer HB 543 (2023) permitless carry; YES long-gun-age-18 bills; NRA-PVF 'A' + endorsement; YES SB 168 (2019) sanctuary-city ban; SB 664 (2020) E-Verify; SB 214 (2023) 2A Financial Privacy Act (anti-federal-tracking); HB 999 (2025) gold/silver legal tender; SB 56 (2025) barring UN climate intervention; co-introducer HB 1 (2023) universal vouchers; endorsed by FL PBA. FRTL PAC endorsed. Note: a Freedom Index entry shows 'NO on HB 1' that conflicts with his co-introducer status (likely an amendment/scoring artifact) -- scored from the primary bill-sponsorship record. NO EVIDENCE on marriage/Christian-liberty -> null.

0.note: YES SB 300 (2023 6-wk heartbeat ban), HB 5 (2022 15-wk ban), SB 404 (2020 parental consent); Florida Right to Life PAC endorsement.

4.note: FRTL PAC endorsed; no abortion-industry PAC funding found.

0.note: Co-introducer of HB 1 (2023) universal school-choice/voucher expansion.

4.note: YES SB 56 (2025) prohibiting UN-backed climate intervention -> opposes supranational/WEF-aligned economic capture.

3.note: YES SB 524 (2022) Election Security Act tightening drop boxes/mail-ballot rules.

0.note: Endorsed by Florida Police Benevolent Association and Suncoast firefighters; affirmative pro-law-enforcement support.

2.note: YES SB 214 (2023) Second Amendment Financial Privacy Act -> resisted federal/financial firearm tracking.
YES SB 168 (2019) banning sanctuary-city policies statewide.

1.note: YES bills lowering long-gun purchase age to 18 (rolling back post-Parkland limit); NRA-PVF 'A' rating + endorsement.

profile.background: Real estate broker (CCIM). First took office FL House HD-75 (Charlotte Co. / Port Charlotte-Punta Gorda) Nov 2024, succeeding term-limited Michael Grant. Sits on Education & Employment and Civil Justice committees.

danny-nix-jr@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06 (state tier). Thin first-term (2024) record scored on documented votes/sponsorships only. HARD: co-introducer + YES vote on CS/HB 289 (2026) fetal personhood; primary sponsor HB 1371 (2025, LEO personnel, became law) and HB 653 (2025, aggravating factors for capital felonies, became law). ATTRIBUTION CAUTION: Vote411 'pro-choice'/pro-red-flag quotes circulating under 'Danny Nix' actually belong to his 2024 Democratic opponent Tony Dunbar -- contradicted by Nix's own pro-life vote; NOT scored against Nix. Marriage/family/elections/border/direct-2A returned NO EVIDENCE (campaign site is slogans only) -> null. Florida Realtors PAC endorsed.

0.note: Named co-introducer and YES vote on CS/HB 289 (2026) fetal personhood/wrongful-death bill.
Primary sponsor HB 1371 (2025) Law Enforcement Officers personnel bill (became law); affirmative pro-LEO.

2.note: Primary sponsor HB 653 (2025) Aggravating Factors for Capital Felonies (became law) -> proportional punishment of evildoers.

profile.background: CEO of Ambitrans (ambulance company); B.A. economics (William & Mary), J.D. (UF Levin). First elected FL House HD-76 (DeSoto + parts of Charlotte & Lee; resides Punta Gorda) Nov 2024, succeeding Spencer Roach. 2026 R primary vs Julie Louise McCormack; Dem Phillip Ray Carter.

vanessa-oliver@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06 (state tier). Freshman (2024). HARD: Florida Right to Life endorsement (2024) + her own statement 'pro-life and will vote no on 4' (2024 abortion-rights amendment). Cited campaign statements: backs state/local law enforcement (+ supported HB 751 2025 LEO benefits), 'deport illegal aliens and secure the border', supported restoring 18-21 long-gun purchase rights. Campaign Issues page is slogan-level on education/economy/federalism -> those null (no specific position). No cross-party deviations.

0.note: Florida Right to Life endorsed; stated 'pro-life and will vote no on 4' (2024 abortion-rights amendment).

4.note: FRTL-endorsed; no abortion-industry PAC funding found.

0.note: Campaign site: 'allow for state and local law enforcement to do their job'; supported HB 751 (2025) LEO/correctional-officer benefits.

1.note: Campaign site stated position: 'deport illegal aliens and secure the border.'
Publicly backed restoring 18-21 long-gun purchase rights (rolling back post-Parkland age limit) -> opposing a gun restriction.

profile.background: Business-chamber executive (President/CEO SWFL Inc.; ex-Chief of Staff Greater Naples Chamber). BA communication + MBA, FGCU. FL House HD-77 (Lee Co. / Bonita Springs-Estero) since 2022. 2026 R primary vs Michael Thompson.

tiffany-esposito@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06 (state tier). DELIBERATELY CLEARED prior uncited party-inference scores. Business-chamber moderate; DEVIATION WATCH -- an unconfirmed news characterization has her reluctant to 'rubber-stamp the GOP agenda' on LGBTQ/gender bills, but it could NOT be confirmed from a primary source, so social cells are NULL (not scored either way), NOT inferred. Kept only HARD-cited cells: sanctity[0] SB 300 (2023 6-wk ban) party-line House passage with no Republican recorded voting no; self_defense[0] HB 543 (2023) permitless carry, party-line 76-32 with the only R 'no' being the named crossover Vicki Lopez (Esposito on the recorded yes side); self_defense[1] NRA endorsement (2022). Economic 'fiscally responsible'/AFP housing-champion framing did not map to the specific state economic cells (CBDC/balanced-budget/usury/anti-ESG) -> null; chamber-CEO background makes anti-ESG/WEF unsupported. Endorsed DeSantis 2024; FL Chamber; NRA.

0.note: FL House SB 300 (2023) 6-week heartbeat ban: party-line passage, no House Republican recorded voting no; Esposito (R) on the recorded yes side.
HB 543 (2023) permitless/constitutional carry passed 76-32 with the only Republican 'no' being named crossover Vicki Lopez; Esposito (R, NRA-endorsed) on the recorded yes side.

1.note: NRA endorsement (2022) -> issue-group HARD evidence opposing gun-control restrictions.
